channel.title: MCP News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated news about Model Context Protocol, AI agents, tool integrations, MCP security, and developer tooling.

item.title: Four rival LLMs, zero consensus: designing an MCP panel
item.description: The obvious version of a multi-model tool is a black box: send it a question, it asks GPT and Gemini and Claude and Grok, and hands you back one blended answer. That was the first thing I cut. I built mcp-second-opinion, a small MCP stdio server that lets an agent like Claude Code or Cursor consult...
item.dc:creator: Akash Hadagali Persetti

item.title: 3 MCP servers I actually use daily (and how to set them up)
item.description: Not a hype list. These are the three MCP servers I have running right now in Claude Desktop. 1. Filesystem mcp-hub install @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem I use this to let Claude read project files directly instead of me copy-pasting code. Configure it to point at your project root. Real...
item.dc:creator: GrahamduesCN

item.title: Membangun MCP Server untuk Data Bisnis Manufaktur: AI Agent Cek Harga dan Stok Real-Time
item.description: Selama setahun terakhir saya membangun sistem operasional untuk beberapa bisnis manufaktur di kawasan industri Karawang, dan satu masalah terus berulang: AI assistant yang dipakai tim sales tidak pernah tahu harga terbaru, sisa stok kain drill, atau posisi order di lini produksi. Jawabannya selalu...
item.dc:creator: Mightyblue

item.title: Git Solved Source Control. Who Solves Project Intelligence?
item.description: Git changed software forever. Before Git, developers lost code. Today, we almost never lose code. But we lose something else every single day. Knowledge. Ask yourself: Why was this module designed like this? Why wasn’t another library chosen? Which assumptions are still valid? What architectural...
item.dc:creator: EvanLin | Contorium

item.title: Coding Agents Don’t Need Bigger Context Windows. They Need Better Context.
item.description: The AI ecosystem is entering a new phase. The discussion is no longer about which model writes better code. It’s about which system can preserve understanding across hours, days, and months of development. The missing layer Most coding agents are stateless. They can inspect files. They can generate...
item.dc:creator: EvanLin | Contorium

item.title: Why the codebase is your biggest context bill, and why output compression doesn't fix it
item.description: When people think about reducing AI coding costs, they usually think about the model's output: fewer words, shorter explanations, tighter responses. It's intuitive. The AI talks a lot. Make it talk less. The problem is that output is the wrong target. Paul Kinlan analysed OpenRouter's programming...
item.dc:creator: foldwork

item.title: QR Code Generator API — 5분 안에 프로젝트에 통합하기
item.description: QR Code Generator API — 5분 안에 프로젝트에 통합하기 이커머스, 출석체크, 메뉴판, 결제링크 — QR 필요한 곳 어디든 프로젝트에 qr code generator 기능이 필요한데 직접 구현하기엔 시간이 아까운가요? REST API 한 줄이면 됩니다. Quick Start # 즉시 테스트 (API 키 불필요) curl https://api.lazy-mac.com/qr-code/ 실전 예제 Python import requests resp =...
item.dc:creator: 2x lazymac

item.title: Describe your website once, make it usable by any AI
item.description: If you have tried to make a website work with AI assistants recently, you have run into the fragmentation. Depending on the platform, you are looking at MCP, WebMCP, ACP, product feeds, OAuth flows, framework-specific tooling, and whatever ships next. Each solves a real slice of the problem. But a...
item.dc:creator: Roland Farkas

item.title: One agent web-fetch tool returned 4.6M tokens for a 3.9MB CSV. Another returned 909.
item.description: TL;DR — I gave the same 3.9 MB Japanese-government CSV to five web-fetch tools that coding agents use. Jina Reader returned 4,593,027 tokens (instant context death). amenbo — my MCP server — returned a header preview plus a pointer to the raw file: 909 tokens. That ~5,000× gap is the headline, but...
item.dc:creator: Rererr

item.title: InsightsTrack + Pulse: I taught Claude Desktop to read my web analytics (via MCP)
item.description: This is a submission for Weekend Challenge: Passion Edition What I Built InsightsTrack is a self-hosted, privacy-first web analytics platform — the open-source alternative to Google Analytics. No cookies, no consent banner, no data selling. You run it on your own server and get real-time visitors,...
item.dc:creator: Nishikanta Ray

item.title: Taiwan GCIS records: fields you only notice mid-investigation
item.description: Taiwan’s GCIS register will often show a company’s status history in Chinese long before English-language databases settle on one transliteration. That turns into a real problem when you are tracing a supplier linked to export controls, chip procurement, or an intermediary that has renamed itself...
item.dc:creator: OpenRegistry

item.title: # Online vs. Offline Password Attacks: A Field Guide
item.description: Password attacks are one of the first things people learn in offensive security, and one of the most commonly muddled. The confusion usually comes down to a single distinction that determines which tool you reach for, how fast you move, and how much noise you make: is the attack online or offline?...
item.dc:creator: auto_majicly

item.title: Agent Runs now available in the Vercel MCP and CLI
item.description: Your agent can now inspect Agent Runs via the Vercel MCP and CLI for eve, the open-source agent framework.eve traces are automatically ingested when deployed to Vercel and available as Agent Runs. The new Vercel MCP tools and Vercel CLI commands let you find projects with runs, list recent runs,...
item.dc:creator: Brooke Mosby

item.title: Announcing the Monetization Gateway: charge for any resource behind Cloudflare via x402
item.description: We're opening the waitlist for our Monetization Gateway, which will allow you to charge for any web page, dataset, API, or MCP tool behind Cloudflare. The charges will settle in stablecoins over the x402 open protocol, with no payments stack of your own to build.
item.dc:creator: Will Papper

item.title: Beta SDKs for the 2026-07-28 MCP Spec Release Candidate Are Here
item.description: Beta releases of the Python, TypeScript, Go, and C# SDKs are now available with support for the 2026-07-28 MCP specification release candidate. Here is what changes for your server, how to migrate, and how to test before the spec goes final on July 28.
item.dc:creator: Model Context Protocol

item.title: MCP for Beginners: Why Every AI Engineer and Developer Should Learn the Model Context Protocol
item.description: If you have spent any time building with large language models in the last year, you have hit the same wall everyone hits: your model is brilliant at reasoning but blind to the real world. It cannot read your database, call your internal API, search your documents, or trigger a deployment unless...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: MCP Server Authorization with Azure API Management: From Simple to Advanced
item.description: Why put API Management in front of your MCP servers The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has quickly become the standard way for AI agents, such as GitHub Copilot in VS Code, to reach external tools and data. As soon as an MCP server does anything meaningful, the same questions that govern any API...
item.dc:creator: vzisiadis

item.title: Enterprise-Managed Authorization: Zero-touch OAuth for MCP
item.description: The Enterprise-Managed Authorization extension to the Model Context Protocol is now stable, enabling organizations to centrally provision MCP server access through their identity provider so users get connected servers on first login without per-app OAuth.
item.dc:creator: Model Context Protocol
